title: Pulling an image based on its digest is security-sensitive
description: This rule is deprecated; use S6596 instead. A container image digest uniquely and immutably identifies a container image. A tag, on the other hand, is a mutable reference to a container image.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Pulling an image based on its digest is security-sensitive. Using tags instead of digests can lead to non-reproducible builds and security vulnerabilities. Tags are mutable and can point to different image versions over time. Use immutable digests (@sha256:...) to ensure you're always using the exact intended image version. Example: FROM ubuntu@sha256:abc123... instead of FROM ubuntu:22.04
